title: Meta-rdk: Raw EEG data
description: This dataset contains raw EEG recordings from a visual discrimination task comparing individuals with schizophrenia spectrum disorders and healthy controls. Participants performed a motion coherence discrimination task with concurrent confidence ratings, using adaptive staircase procedures to equalize task difficulty across groups. The study investigates neural correlates of perceptual decision-making and metacognitive processes in psychotic disorders.
methods_description: EEG data were collected during a visual discrimination task involving 100 moving dots presented at the center of the screen. Participants indicated perceived motion direction (left/right) via mouse clicks on targets at screen corners. Motion coherence was individually adapted using a 1up/2down staircase procedure. Following each decision, participants rated confidence on a visual analog scale (0-100%). The study included 20 individuals with schizophrenia spectrum disorder and 22 healthy controls.
